turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Dad, where are we off to?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: First we will go to the city centre and stop for something to drink. Then we will visit the University Museum.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Where are we going to have a drink?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: There is a coffee shop round the corner. Can you see that big building at the end of the road?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That is a bank. The coffee shop is opposite the bank.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Good. I will have hot chocolate.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: A stop! Wait for the lights to turn green.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: When crossing the road you must always pay attention to what's around you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sorry. How far is the museum?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you watch television last night, Charles?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I did.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It was a good game, wasn't it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, I didn't watch the football match. I wanted to but my wife preferred to see the old film.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What a pity. It was quite exciting. Both teams played very well.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: How did it finish?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It finished in a draw. What was the film like?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It was quite good. But. I missed the beginning of it because I had to eat first.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did your wife enjoy it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, she didn't. After half an hour she stopped watching and started to read a book.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hi, this is Maria. Is that Shirley?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi, Maria, this is Shirley.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Shirley, do you know today's homework from our economic law class? I have written it on a piece of paper but I can't find it now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hold on for a moment please. I'm fetching my textbook.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, the homework is to explain what economic law is both in broad sense and in narrow sense.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Explain the economic law? An essay question again?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. You can use your textbook for your reference.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Are there any clear answers in the book to this question?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: ABC Rent-a-Car. May I help you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I would like to rent a car. What kind of car do you have?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: We have Volkswagen, Pinto, Plymouth and Datsun.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is the rate?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Twenty dollars per day for Volkswagen, Pinto and Datsun, and twenty-seven dollars for Plymouth.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: How about mileage and gas?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There is no charge for mileage. And fill up the gas when you return the car to us as it is not included.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK. I'll try a Pinto. I want to rent a car for one week.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: All right, sir. May I have your driver's license?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Here you are.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Could you talk to me for a few minutes about my grades?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Step into my office for a moment. What is your concern?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Am I failing?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Let's go on the computer to see what's up. Do you think you are doing well?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I am pretty sure that I am in trouble.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I can see that you are trying, but where do you think you could improve?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I missed a few classes; I need to make sure I come to school on a regular basis.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Having a regular study routine and following it is very important, right?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'll try; it's hard!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Thanks for stopping by!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The weather in Beijing is just beautiful!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Autumn is the best season in Beijing, you know.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, I can see. The summer heat is over, and the winter cold is still far away.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is the weather like in Washington at this time of this year?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Autumn is also very nice in Washington.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Is it very cold in winter there?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, it isn't. But sometimes it snows a lot in winter.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Does it rain a lot in summer there?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh yes! Sometimes it rains very heavily. How about here in Beijing?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: There's a lot of rain in summer, but it seldom rains in autumn.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What sports do you like to play?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like baseball and basketball.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How about football?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: In my country football is brand new, so I never learned the game when I was younger.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's a very popular game in America.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah. I heard a lot about it. Is it really that fun?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: For me, it's one of those games that are fun to both watch and play.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Are you a football player?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, I just for fun with my friends. We play two hand touch or flag football. We're too old to play tackle football.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is two hand touch and flag football?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Are the children all grown up now, Rose?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, yes. Laura's the oldest. She's a computer programmer.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, very interesting. And what about Rita? She was such a bright child — always reading.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She's a librarian at the public library.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Really? And Chris? She was a very pretty little girl.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Christy is working as a waitress at a restaurant in Paris. She's married to a French photographer.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, how interesting. And what about Larry and Rocky?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Larry drives a truck. He drives all over the country. We hardly ever seen him.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Really? Where does he live now?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Colorado.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi, Is that David?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hi, Jenny. It's David here.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, what's new?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Not much. My girlfriend just dumped me.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Sorry to hear that. What happened?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, she told me things just weren't working out and we weren't right together. But I thought things were just great. Why did she say this? It's completely out of the blue.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, that's not the girl with pink hair, is it? The girl I saw with Jack yesterday.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What? She was cheating on me with that ugly guy. Oh, no, I feel like such a loser.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, she is not worth it. Forget her.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But she lied to me.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Steven, would you like to go dance with us tonight?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: John, I am just not in the mood for this.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You look so upset. What's going on?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I lost the table tennis game yesterday.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, what a pity!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I just don't want to play table tennis any more.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is it that bad? It's nothing more than a game.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: My opponent bowled me with the very first ball. I was wondering if it's appropriate for me to play table tennis.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, come on! Failure is the mother of success. Don't lose heart. I'm sure you'll succeed.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Perhaps you are right. But I still need some time to recover from the failure.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I hear you bought a new house in the northern suburbs.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's right, we bought it the same day we came on the market.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What kind of house is it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's a wonderful Spanish style.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, I love the roof tiles on Spanish style houses.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And it's a bargaining. A house like this in river side costs double the price.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great, is it a two bedroom house?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, it has three bedrooms and three beds, and has a living room with a twelve-foot ceiling. There's a two-car garage.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's a nice area too. It'll be a good investment for you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeas, when will you buy a house?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Aren't you going to swim?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Are you kidding? Don't you know I can't swim?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You are not telling me that we drove 50 miles to come here for just sunbath, are you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hey, don't shout. Come here! Sit closer. Now, Jim, we'Ve had a lovely day. Don't spoil it now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What about your beautiful bathing suit? We shopped all day. Don't you want to show it off?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Everybody who walks past me sees it. Besides, it's a good sunbathing suit.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: All right. Suit yourself. I am going swimming.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Jim!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Can you get me a beach umbrella and a deck chair? I am really tired of lying in thousand.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I feel absolutely horrible. My temperature is 41 degrees Celsius, and I've got a headache and a runny nose.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: do you have any other symptoms?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've also got a terrible stomach-ache. Is my face still swollen?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: just a little. Has your toothache gone now?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: yes, for the most part. It doesn't feel as bad as my other ailments, anyway.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: how about your tongue? Does it still hurt?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: no, the burn ointment seemed to take effect right away. I think it's already healed.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: how did you get that burn again?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I scalded on the hot coffee a few days ago.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: you haven't had much luck lately, have you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey Mike. What are you doing?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Nothing much. What are you up to?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I was just concerned about Sam. He hasn't been himself lately.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: He took the civil service exam and failed.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That sucks. He must feel depressed.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah. He's been sitting in his room everyday for the last 4 days.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why don't we take him out? We can try to take his mind off of it. In the least show him that we're there for him.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's a great idea. Why don't you call him. I already talked to him a couple of times and it might be good for him to hear from somebody else.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ok. I'll call you back after I'm done.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sounds good.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Have you been ever been to Disneyworld?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: When I was young, my parents took me to the one in California. I always forget which — is that one Disneyland or Disneyworld?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: The one in Florida is Disneyworld and the one in California is Disneyland, so you must have been to Disneyland.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's right. Why do you ask?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, I've never been to either so I was thinking of going to Disneyworld for my honeymoon.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: When are getting married?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Some time next year. We haven't set the date yet.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Congratulations! I had no idea.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thank you. So what do you think? Would we have a good time?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you like amusement parks?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Mary, how was your date with john?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: it's ok. It seems we have a lot in common.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: oh, really. That is great news. What does he look like?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: he is tall and slim, fair-haired.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: sounds like he is pretty cute. What do you think of him?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: he is a nice guy and very considerate. I was impressed with how smart he was and he has a great sense of humor.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: oh, it's love at first sight. When will you see each other again?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: he didn't say, but he said he would call me.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: maybe he is busy with his work. Just wait and try not to think about it too much!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: oh, steve. Stop it! I am a bit nervous! What if he doesn't call?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Excuse me, what's the screen near your steering wheel for?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's a portable TV. It's a popular thing now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, that's new to me. So what's on everyday?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: News about current affairs, documentaries, music, movies, noncommercial ads and so on.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is there anything interesting?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, there are something good and informative. I think that many people underestimate the value of TV in education.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I agree. Are there any commercials on the TV?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course. Because the TV stations need to make money from commercials between the programs.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That makes a lot of sense. Does the TV work well?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Not always. It depends on the transmission of the satellite signals.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: hey, Maris, are you a part-timer?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: part-timer? Is that a new buzzword?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: oh, it's pretty popular among my classmates these days. So are you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: sure, I have a part-time job as a bartender.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: cool! It must be amazing to work at a bar!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: yeah, but I'm not there for fun. I have to remain professional and if I get distracted, the manager would be pretty upset.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: that's true. I'm wondering did you learn anything from the job?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: of course. There's always much to learn. I got to know more about the real world.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: you mean you got working experience, right?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: not just working experience. I would say the biggest lesson I learned is how to deal with people from all walks of life.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I have a real problem when I meet people.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is it? Maybe I can help.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I never know when to shake hands and when not to. Can you tell me something about it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you come from a country where they shake hands?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh yes. Men, women, and children shake hands all the time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's very simple. Men shake hands, women and children don't.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: But some women do shake hands. And now a lot of children have such good manners, they shake hands too.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, a good rule to follow is, for men you hold out your hand, for women and children, you wait and see what they do.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That sounds easy enough.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure. Once you get the hang of it, it's a piece of cake! It's nothing to worry about.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hello, Jim. What was the film like?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Awful. It was a complete waste of time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why? What was it about?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It was about a married couple. They had to live with the wife's mother, because they didn't have enough money to buy a house of their own.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: A lot of young people have to do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes. but the husband had to work overtime three times a week, so he was always tired.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: lt sounds like the story of my life.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, it does, doesn't it? But this man was always over-tired, and he couldn't sleep. So he used to take two sleeping pill every night.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I take sleeping pills sometimes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, but not two every night. Anyway, the strain was too much for him. He had nervous breakdown and had to go to hospital.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Which church do you attend?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I am Mormon, so I go to the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the difference between this and other Churches?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Mormons believe that, after the death of Christ and the death of the Apostles 10, their church Christ founded became corrupt and the authority of Christ on earth was lost to men. In 1830, a prophet named Joseph Smith was brought up by God to restore the church.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Then does that mean the other churches are corrupt?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That means that they all have parts of the original gospel, but not all.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you believe that Mormons have all of it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why do you really go to church?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: We gather together to encourage each other, because we have common beliefs. We come together and call it church.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How do you like your job here, Linda?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's interesting, I like being an editor.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like talking to writers, I get to meet a lot of interesting people.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'm happy to hear that, but publishing is more than just talking to writers. it involves a lot of work, patience and responsibility. Most of the work is boring.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't feel bored at all. I'm getting along with my colleagues. I will enjoy working with them. People are great in this industry.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you have any complaints?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, I have no complaints. I hear there are many opportunities to move up in this company. is that true?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, you have many opportunities to grow in this company as long as you do well.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's perfect. I will definitely do well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Mike, come and look at this painting of shrimp! It is so simple yet so vivid!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah. It is incredible! Is it the work of Qi Bakshi?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, it is. How do you know that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, Qi is famous all over the world, isn't he? Besides I am quite interested in Chinese painting.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Cool! What kind of Chinese painting do you prefer, free sketch or claborate-style painting?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I prefer free sketch, especially the landscape paintings. I can always feel peace and harmony from those landscape paintings.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's true. Chinese art stresses the harmony between man and nature, which is an important part of China's traditional culture.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I love this art exhibition!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: So do I. Let's go to other areas to see what they have got, shall we?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Doris, I'm glad you're home. I'm terrified. I don't know what to do!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What is it? What happened?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I think someone is stalking me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, it can't be. Really? Who?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I don't know. I saw him the first time Tuesday. He was at the cafe. I noticed he was looking at me a lot. Not just the usual looking, but staring. He just kept staring at me. He didn't stop.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What did you do?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I didn't do anything. Finally, he left. But then I saw him again today. Outside the shoe store. Near the cafe. I went into a CD store and pretended I was looking at CD's. But then he came in too.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Did he leave when you left?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. Then I noticed he was on the sidewalk behind me. He was following me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What did you do?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: well, I finished my last final today.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: the end of all the hard work for my master's. what a nice feeling to get my degree!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do you want to attend the convocation?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: certainly. After years of hard work, I wouldn't miss it. By the way, where can I find cap and gown?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do you want to have them made or do you want to rent them?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: oh, I think they're provided by the school for that special day.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: no. those you have to provide for yourself.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: what do most of the students do?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: well, most of them only need a cap and gown for that particular convocation service, but some of the education majors have had them made, because they will be faculty members, and they'll need them f

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: then, I might as well have them made.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: how do you usually get to school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I usually ride my bike. Which form of transport do you prefer to use?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I feel that I can see more when I pedal a bicycle, but when I feel lazy, I drive my car.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I think we should discourage people from using their private cars. They produce too much pollution!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I agree, but I would find it difficult to stop using my car. It's just so convenient.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: cars might be convenient, but they're so bad for the environment.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do you have a car?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: no. I used to have one though. Once I started using my bicycle to get around, I found that I didn't really need it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: maybe if I sold my car now, I wouldn't be so tempted to use it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: you could try. It would save you a lot of money.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Say, Jim, how about going for a few beers after dinner?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You know that is tempting but is really not good for our fitness.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What do you mean? It will help us to relax.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you really think so? I don't. It will just make us fat and act silly. Remember last time?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I guess you are right. But what shall we do? I don't feel like sitting at home.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I suggest a walk over to the gym where we can play singsong and meet some of our friends.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's a good idea. I hear Mary and Sally often go there to play pingpong. Perhaps we can make a foursome with them.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sounds great to me! If they are willing, we could ask them to go dancing with us. That is excellent exercise and fun, too.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Good. Let' s go now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: All right.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: is everything alright?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: yes, everything is fine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: what took so long? I thought your flight was supposed to arrive 3 hours ago!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: didn't they announce that our flight was delayed?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I didn't hear anything about a delay. I thought everything was running on time. What happened?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: we boarded the plane on time, but then we were held up for almost an hour due to a maintenance problem.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: then what? Your plane was three hours late!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: we finally took off, but about 30 minutes later, the captain came on the loudspeaker to warn us that there would be some bad turbulence for most of the flight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: turbulence is pretty normal, isn't it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: yes, but this was the worst turbulence I'd ever experienced in my life! Everyone started getting sick. The flight attendants had to stay in their seats. The captain decided to land early.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: why are you so upset today, Maria? Cheer up!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: oh, Iris, it's about the job with the PR company.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: it's a great opportunity. You should be happy with the offer.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: but my mother won't allow me to accept any part-time job. I'm afraid she will say no again.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: why? It's unfair. You can learn a lot from it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: she says a part-time job is cursed blessing. It takes so much time and energy that I won't be able to focus on my studys.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: that may be true. But I'm sure you're able to find a balance between them. Why not try it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I turned down quite a few offers before for her sake. But I'm a senior now, I don't want to miss the chance this time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I agree with you but I think you really need to talk to your mother and tell her what's on your mind.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'll try, but what if she insists?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: does your family have a record of your ancestors?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: sure. My mom has been working on our family tree for years. She's always updating it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do you have a copy of your family tree in your house? I'd love to see it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I can show it to you now, if you like. I think it goes back about 8 generations so far.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: that's amazing. Do you have a large extended family?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'Ve got 30 cousins on my mom's side and 10 cousins on my dad's side.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: are you very close to your first-cousins?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: the ones that are my age are close relatives. Now that I'm older, I don't spend as much time with them as I used to, so I don't know my younger cousins as well as the older ones.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I see. who's the head of your household?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: it's definitely my mother. And, her mother is definitely the matriarch of the bigger family.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Ok, so who are we going to invite to our dinner party?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, we have the Thompsons, the Greens, and the Anderson, about twelve people total.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Don't forget my friend Andy from the office. He has nothing to do on Friday, so I invited him over.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But that makes thirteen people! We can't have thirteen people for dinner on Friday!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why can't we?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's an old superstition. If we have thirteen people at the table, then it means bad luck. Can Andy bring a date?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'm not sure. He just broke up with his girlfriend recently, and I don't think he's in the mood to start again so soon.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But we can't have thirteen for dinner!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I think you're making a mountain out of a molehill. It's only a superstition. It really doesn't mean anything.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Absolutely not! I refuse to have thirteen people for dinner on Friday!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey, This is your order.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Are you new here? I come to this coffeehouse often. But I didn't see you here before.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. I started this part-time job just three days ago. And my internship will last 1 week. I'm still a student now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Do you mind if I ask you why you've taken the part-time job here?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Of course not. You know, there are many foreign customers like you here. And I think it's good for me to improve my oral English.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, are you an English student? Or you just study English for interest's sake?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, I'm not an English student. But I do like English. I'm going to be a volunteer in the 2008 Olympics where I want to be helpful with the language. So I try to improve my English.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Very nice. Thank you for your time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thank you, sir. But I'm not allowed to accept any tips.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: OK then. I don't want to put you in trouble.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Sam, I am so sorry. It was your birthday yesterday and I completely forgot about it.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Don't worry about it. I keep forgetting everyone's birthdays as well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Margaret really told me off when she found out that I had forgotten all about it. We did try to phone you, but you and Jane must have gone out.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Jane invited me for dinner to this new restaurant which opened near us.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, yes, I know the one. How was the food?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The food was excellent, but Jane ordered a birthday cake, which they forgot about. So as you can see, you were not the only one.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Oh, no.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Jane was quite upset about it. The manager came to apologize and we did get free dessert, but still ...

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I would think this was the least they could do.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: In the end it was a nice evening and I got an iPad as my birthday present which I wanted for a while.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: are you still coming to my place for dinner tomorrow night?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: of course. Is the dinner still on?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: yes, I was just wondering how you and your roommate were planning on coming to my place.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: we were planning on walking both ways since the weather is still nice.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: that's what I thought you would do. Listen, I live in a bit of a rough neighborhood. It's just down the street from all the bars. You probably don't want to be walking around after dark.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: it can't be that bad.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I wish it wasn't, but there is actually a lot of crime and prostitution around here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: really? I never would have guessed. The criminals must only come out in the evenings, because I've never noticed anything strange when I've been at your house in the daytime.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: do me a favor, and take a taxi. It'd make me feel a lot better.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: ok, we will. How do you get around in the evenings?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I've been so stressed these days.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What's the matter? Are you OK?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I don't know where to go for college, and what to major in.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Don't worry. You have plenty of time to decide.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And I also want to get scholarships to help me pay for the tuition. I want to get the burden off my family.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You always worry too much about everything. Take it easy, honey. You are good enough. You can go to the ideal college.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: OK, OK. I'm so sorry. By the way, for you, is it more important to make money or to enjoy your work?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: In a perfect world, I would enjoy my work if I do not have to do much, and make lots of money!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: OK, honey, stop dreaming. You should face the reality.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's only a joke. To be honest, I have to improve my English, because my English is not good enough. I still get confused about some simple grammars. Since you are quite good at it, do you have any suggestions?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey Mike. I forgot about registration. I'm a day late, so all the classes are mostly full. What do you think I should do?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You're screwed. You can't do anything about that. You have to hope that you get some classes that will be useful.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you think going to the registration building will help at all?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No. They will tell you the same thing in a worse way.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you register yet?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course. Registering for classes is not something you want to miss.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What classes do you think are still open?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, I know psychology 101 is a big class, so there will always be seats in that class. You can also get into Sociology.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's helpful. Thanks. But what do you think about philosophy. I wanted to take that class this semester.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I took that class last year. The professor is really cool, so if you go to his office, you can have him sign a card that will let you in even if the class is full.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi, Becky, what's up?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Not much, except that my mother-in-law is driving me up the wall.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the problem?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She loves to nit-pick and criticizes everything that I do. I can never do anything right when she's around.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: For example?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, last week I invited her over to dinner. My husband and I had no problem with the food, but if you listened to her, then it would seem like I fed her old meat and rotten vegetables. There's just nothing can please her.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, I can't see that happening. I know you're a good cook and nothing like that would ever happen.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's not just that. She also criticizes how we raise the kids.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: My mother-in-law used to do the same thing to us. If it wasn't disciplining them enough, then we were disciplining them too much. She also complained about the food we fed them, the schools we sent them too, and everything else under the sun.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You said she used to? How did you stop her?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Today more and more people advocate lifelong learning. But people have different views about lifelong learning. How do you understand lifelong learning?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I think lifelong learning is the concept that it's never too soon or too late for learning

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Does that mean studying all the time?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't think so. I think it's about your attitude towards learning. One should be open to new ideas, decisions, skills or behaviors.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Then the axiom -' You can't teach an old dog new tricks' doesn't work any longer.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You're right. Lifelong learning is about finding learning opportunities at all ages and in numerous contexts

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, I agree with you. When I retired I will participate in programs to keep momentally active.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Me too. To keep up with the changing world, one must be involved in lifelong learning and be ready to accept new things.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You know what? I've recently been teaching myself French.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? That's great! I know that your company has a lot of clients in France.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the life expectancy in your country?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm not sure, but probably about 75 years. How about in your country?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: About 70, I think. This newspaper article talks about the problems of an aging population. It's a problem that will soon affect most of the world.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I heard that the government might need to increase the retirement age, because otherwise there will not be enough workers to support the young and the elderly.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Perhaps we need to have more babies! Tina gave birth to a baby boy yesterday.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Did she? That's great. However, if we have too many children, that will have a bad effect on the enviroment.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How's your son these days?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, he's fine. Kids seem to grow up very quickly nowadays.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: He'll be a teenager before you know it! Teenagers are often rebellious! When do you mine it is a good age to have a child?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I had mine when I was 24. that's a little young. I'd suggest you wait until you are in your late twenties. , or even in your early thirties if you have a good career.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the life expectancy in your country?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm not sure, but probably about 75 years. How about in your country?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I had mine when I was 24. that's a little young. I'd suggest you wait until you are in your late twenties. , or even in your early thirties if you have a good career.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What upsets you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: My parents called. As usual, they reminded me again that I should have a plan to marry by my late 20s. Easier said then done. Who should I marry? I have no time to go on a date.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It is not your mother finding one for you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I will find one myself, of course. I'm a modern girl.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Perhaps you can try the three minutes date, the latest type.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You mean dozens of the opposite sex meet each other for three minutes in a dimly bar serving alcohol, I hate that idea.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: No, there is an updated virgin, three minutes video date. I know an online dating website providing such service with a microphone and webcam, you can sigh for it. You can be face-to-face with a guy talking for maximum three minutes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I don't think it makes sense. Three minutes is such a short time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I think you can find out if there is a possibility of romance within the first second of meeting someone, so-called love at first sight.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Anyway, I don't want to post my face up for sale on the internet like that.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hi Cody, how did practicing go this week?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well I had several tests and an oral presentation this week so I didn't get a chance to memorize the second page, but I think I mastered the tricky section.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great! Warm up with some scales and arpeggios first. Good, good. This week, work on keeping the rhythm steady when you play the last part with the sixteenth note. Now let's take a look at this

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Charles? Before I start I was wondering if it was ok if I put a small crescendo in here and then decrescendo back to pianissimo again over here?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It might work. I'll have to hear it. Show me what you'Ve done.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It was horrible! I played play it much better at home!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's just nerves. Just play the right hand for now. One two three four five six, ta ti tri-ple-ti. Good, good. Don't forget the accidentals! The key signature says that note should be a G-sharp b

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Is that better?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, much better. Watch where you lift your foot off the pedal. What was that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sorry! The stretch for that octave is always hard to make.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's the plot of your new movie?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's a story about a policemen who is investigating a series of strange murders. I play the part of the detective. He has to catch the killer, but there's very little evidence. It's a psychological th

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you enjoy making the movie? We heard stories of disagreement with other actors and with the director.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I have had disagreement with every director. I'Ve worked with. We'Ve always disagreed in a friendly way and we have always resolved our differences. It was the same when I made this movie. I don't no

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Critics are not very happy with the movies that you'Ve made recently. Does that bother you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Not at all. The feedback from audience has been great. I care about what they think more than what the critics think.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you do you own stunts in the movie?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I wanted to, but my insurance company wouldn't let me. All of my stunts were done by a stuntman. As you know, I used to do my own stunts, but I'll leave that to the experts in future.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thank you very much for doing this interview.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: My pleasure. Have you seen the movie yet?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hello! What are you reading about in the newspaper?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Hello! I was exhausted some studying, so I decided to read the newspaper to relax. Unfortunately, the news is so depressing. There has been another murder in the city center. I'm shocked that the police haven't caught the killer yet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: People are starting to get frightened by it. Everyone will be relieved when they finally catch the murder.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: You mean “ if ” they catch the murderer. I'm scared stiff about going into the city center at night.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There must have been some good news in the newspaper. I can't believe that none of the news stories make you happy or excited.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, there was one good piece of news. You remember the local girl who was dying of a rare blood disease?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. Her parents were raising money to have her treated in the united states.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Well, they'Ve got the money and she's going tomorrow for treatment.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I'm so happy for the family! They must be very relieved and excited about that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm sure they are. Oh, and a local man won the lottery. I'm so jealous! I wish it were me! I buy a lottery ticket every week and I'm amazed that I haven't even won a small prize yet. It's so unfair!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Han Maimed, What's your plan for the weekend?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I plan to travel around Harvard University and know more about it. To tell you the truth, I plan to apply for Harvard after graduation from high school. It's better to get to know it. What about you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I get together with my Christian brothers on Saturdays.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Oh, my God! John, I didn't know you are religious. You just look like a scientific kook, like Leonard in the Big Bang Theory.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Come on, Han Maimed, don't make a fuss. A lot of Americans, including many scientists, believe in religion.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: But I'm really surprised. I thought science and religion are not compatible, are they?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, they are compatible. Actually, the U. S. is both the most developed and the most religious country in the world.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I've heard about it a little bit before, but I don't understand. How can people believe in God since they know God doesn't exist?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's a little complicated. You'll get to know it when you are acquainted with the U. S. history later on. Religion was and is a part of American people's life. Guess what? Even Harvard University, which is a highly academic institution, is in a sense the product of religion.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's up? You don't look too good.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah, my head hurts, that's all. I'Ve been in physics class all day. It's killer!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I liked physics. It's all math, really. arcs, curves, velocity, cool stuff.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yeah, yeah, but today's lesson was all about the creation of the universe.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: A physics class about the creation of the universe? That's some pretty unscientific language there. Sounds more religious to me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's all religion. Take the theory of the Big Bang. How is it possible that all of the stuff in the universe comes from an explosion? That's no better than Atlas carrying the globe on his back or African myths about turtles and stuff.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Turtles? Whatever... Look, all that's required for the creation of matter an imbalance of particles and anti-particles. At least, that's what the math says.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Math, sheath. What's the evidence?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There is evidence! You know Edwin Hubble? He's the guy who in the early twentieth century was the first scientist to measure the drift of matter in the universe, thus advancing notions of an expanding universe. What would it be expanding from? Well, the Big Bang... DUH!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Anyway, it's just a theory. Why do people go around touting theories? Where's the scientific rigor in that?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What's up? You don't look too good.

turns.english: Math, sheath. What's the evidence?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: There is evidence! You know Edwin Hubble? He's the guy who in the early twentieth century was the first scientist to measure the drift of matter in the universe, thus advancing notions of an expanding universe. What would it be expanding from? Well, the Big Bang... DUH!

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Anyway, it's just a theory. Why do people go around touting theories? Where's the scientific rigor in that?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Hey Lydia, what are you reading?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'm looking at my horoscope for this month! My outlook is very positive. It says that I should take a vacation to someplace exotic, and that I will have a passionate summer fling!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What are you talking about? Let me see that... What are horoscopes?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It's a prediction of your month, based on your zodiac sign. You have a different sign for the month and date you were born in. I was born on April 15th, so I'm an Aries. When were you born?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: January 5th.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Let's see. . . you're a Capricorn. It says that you will be feeling stress at work, but you could see new, exciting developments in your love life. Looks like we'll both have interesting summers!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That's bogus. I don't feel any stress at work, and my love life is practically nonexistent. This zodiac stuff is all a bunch of nonsense.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No, it's not, your astrology sign can tell you a lot about your personality. See? It says that an Aries is energetic and loves to socialize.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, you certainly match those criteria, but they're so broad they could apply to anyone. What does it say about me?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: A Capricorn is serious-minded and practical. She likes to do things in conventional ways. That sounds just like you!

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: It's so relaxing, taking a walk in the country. The air is so fresh and clean.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: would you like to live in the country? I'm not sure I'd like it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I can see a lot of advantage and disadvantage. The problem is that, for me, each advantage has its own disadvantage.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: What do you mean?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Well, I hate the noise in the city, but I love being around lost of people. The problem is that you can't have lots of people and have peace and quiet. The two just don't go together.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I see what you mean. I love being far away from a city, but I hate being so far away department stores and sports facilities.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: People can't have it both ways. If you live in the country, it is often less convenient. If you live in a city, it is noisy, but there's more to do.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I would love to be surrounded by hills and streams. They're so much better to look at than concrete, rows of parked cars and tall buildings.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I would love to hear the birds singing and feel the fresh breeze on my face. When the wind blows in the city, you get dust in your mouth and in your eyes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The view from the hill is so beautiful and relaxing. There's so sign of pollution. The village looks so peaceful.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What kind of music do you like listening to?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I like music that has a fast beat and is lively, like dance music. You know, I go to a disco almost every week. Sometimes it's too loud though. You prefer classical music, don't you?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, I do. I find it very relaxing. I often listen to Mozart or Bach in the evening after a hard day at work.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I must admit that I like several pieces of classical music. It's certainly more sophisticated that modern dance music.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Classical music is supposed to be good for you brain. Research suggest that it makes your brain more active. Students who listen to classical music while studying perform better.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Really? Perhaps I should listen to classical music often. I heard that listening to classical music is helpful in reducing stress.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes. That's why I listen to it in the evenings. I usually play it as background music while I'm cooking or doing other housework.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: I'Ve got a few classical music CD's. I should follow your lead and increase my brian power.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: You can find plenty of recording on the internet too. You can listen to samples and then buy them very cheaply if you like them.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: That's a good idea. You should do the same with some music. You might find something you like. Classical music might make you clever, but dance might make you livelier and happier.
